notes: Silver rules for base.bls_oews. Silver is a 1:1 promotion of bronze.bls_oews with grain hash (record_id) and validation. All Bronze invariants must continue to hold. Monotonicity tightened to 100% per spec (Bronze already at 100% per EDA -- no rows are filtered or transformed in a way that could break ordering). Same DELIBERATE EXCLUSION as Bronze: no `wage_annual_mean <= wage_annual_p90` rule (23 top-coded SOCs report mean > p90 by BLS design).

rules.name: Grain uniqueness: soc_code
rules.sql: SELECT soc_code, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.bls_oews GROUP BY soc_code HAVING cnt > 1
rules.description: The declared grain is one row per detailed soc_code. Any duplicate indicates a transformer or promote failure. This is the structural foundation of the Silver table and the join key for Gold enrichment.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 2 DQ Rules: 'No duplicate soc_code values (P0).' Bronze RAW-OEWS-003 already validates this; the Silver promote pattern (compute_grain_id with prefix 'oews') preserves uniqueness 1:1 from Bronze. EDA: '831 distinct (100% unique).'

rules.name: SOC code format: 100% match XX-XXXX
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_oews WHERE soc_code IS NULL OR NOT regexp_matches(soc_code, '^\d{2}-\d{4}$')
rules.description: 100% of rows must have soc_code matching the SOC 2018 standard format XX-XXXX. Silver enforces structural validity; Bronze bypasses are not permitted at this layer.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 2 DQ Rules: 'SOC code format: 100% match \d{2}-\d{4} (P0).' Bronze RAW-OEWS-002 also validates; Silver re-asserts because the spec elevates the format invariant from 'no violations' (Bronze) to '100% match' (Silver) -- they are equivalent at zero violations but the Silver framing makes it the table-level contract.

rules.name: Row count within expected range: 800-900
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN cnt < 800 OR cnt > 900 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.bls_oews)
rules.description: Silver is a 1:1 promotion of Bronze -- no rows added or dropped. Row count must remain in [800, 900], matching the Bronze count expectation (~830 detailed SOCs).
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 2 DQ Rules: 'All Bronze DQ rules still pass (P0).' Bronze RAW-OEWS-001 holds row count in [800, 900]. The Silver promotion is 1:1 (no filtering, no aggregation) per spec §Zone 2 transformations.

rules.name: wage_annual_median non-null rate >= 99%
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN (CAST(non_null AS DOUBLE) / CAST(total AS DOUBLE)) < 0.99 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, SUM(CASE WHEN wage_annual_median IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS non_null FROM base.bls_oews)
rules.description: wage_annual_median must be non-null for at least 99% of rows. Carried from Bronze RAW-OEWS-004 (tightened from spec's 95%); Silver re-asserts as part of 'all Bronze rules still pass'.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 2 DQ Rules: 'All Bronze DQ rules still pass (P0).' Bronze RAW-OEWS-004 tightened from spec's 95% to 99% per EDA evidence (actual 99.398%). The Silver promote does not transform wage values, so the same threshold applies.

rules.name: 100% monotonicity: p10 <= p25 <= median <= p75 <= p90
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_oews WHERE wage_annual_p10 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p25 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_median IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p75 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p90 IS NOT NULL AND NOT (wage_annual_p10 <= wage_annual_p25 AND wage_annual_p25 <= wage_annual_median AND wage_annual_median <= wage_annual_p75 AND wage_annual_p75 <= wage_annual_p90)
rules.description: 100% of rows with full annual wage data must satisfy p10 <= p25 <= median <= p75 <= p90. Silver explicitly enforces monotonicity per spec (Silver transformation §2). Any violation rejects the promote.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 2 DQ Rules: 'Monotonicity: 100% of rows with full wage data satisfy p10 <= p25 <= median <= p75 <= p90 (P0).' Spec §Silver Transformations: 'Verify p10 <= p25 <= median <= p75 <= p90 (where non-null). Log violations.' EDA: '826/826 rows -- zero violations.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: record_id not null and unique
rules.sql: SELECT record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.bls_oews GROUP BY record_id HAVING cnt > 1 UNION ALL SELECT 'NULL_CHECK' AS record_id, COUNT(*) AS cnt FROM base.bls_oews WHERE record_id IS NULL HAVING cnt > 0
rules.description: record_id is the deterministic surrogate primary key computed via compute_grain_id(row, ['soc_code'], prefix='oews'). It must be non-null and unique across all rows. SHA-256 hash is collision-free for 831 distinct soc_codes.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 2 Schema: 'record_id: Deterministic grain hash (prefix oews), required.' Spec §Promote pattern: 'compute_grain_id(row, [soc_code], prefix=oews).' Mirrors SLV-OOH-007 from base.bls_ooh which has been passing for 832 rows.
rules.evidence_link: governance/eda/raw-bls-oews-eda.md §Domain Context -> Grain (831 distinct SOC codes)

rules.name: Per-row monotonicity (Bronze invariant carried forward)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_oews WHERE wage_annual_p25 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_median IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p75 IS NOT NULL AND NOT (wage_annual_p25 <= wage_annual_median AND wage_annual_median <= wage_annual_p75)
rules.description: Per-row p25 <= median <= p75 invariant from Bronze RAW-OEWS-005, restated at Silver to ensure the inner-quartile chain is unbroken even in rows where p10 or p90 are null. This is a strict subset of SLV-OEWS-005 but runs on more rows (does not require p10/p90 non-null).
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 2 DQ Rules: 'All Bronze DQ rules still pass.' The Bronze chain in RAW-OEWS-005 only checks rows with all five percentiles non-null; this rule checks any row where the three inner percentiles are non-null, providing a tighter floor for the realistic 'distribution display' band (p25-p75). EDA shows 826 rows pass the full chain and the same 826 pass the inner chain (suppression is all-or-nothing per the entertainment-arts cluster).

rules.name: wage_capped flag invariant carried forward
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_oews WHERE (wage_capped = TRUE AND NOT (wage_annual_p10 = 239200 OR wage_annual_p25 = 239200 OR wage_annual_median = 239200 OR wage_annual_p75 = 239200 OR wage_annual_p90 = 239200)) OR (wage_capped = FALSE AND (wage_annual_p10 = 239200 OR wage_annual_p25 = 239200 OR wage_annual_median = 239200 OR wage_annual_p75 = 239200 OR wage_annual_p90 = 239200))
rules.description: wage_capped is True if and only if at least one annual percentile equals the BLS top-code floor of $239,200. This is a biconditional: True without 239200 is a flag-derivation bug; 239200 without True is a missed top-code parse. Spec §Silver Transformation §3: 'Where wage_capped = True, ensure the capped percentile(s) are exactly 239200.'
rules.rationale: Spec §Silver Transformations §3 plus §Zone 2 DQ Rules ('All Bronze rules still pass'). Combines Bronze RAW-OEWS-006 and RAW-OEWS-007 into the biconditional invariant. EDA: 45 capped SOCs all satisfy the biconditional, 0 violations either direction.

rules.name: occupation_title not null and not empty
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_oews WHERE occupation_title IS NULL OR TRIM(occupation_title) = ''
rules.description: occupation_title is a required field. Carried from Bronze (RAW-OEWS-010) verbatim through the Silver promote.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 2 Schema: 'occupation_title: yes (required).' Bronze EDA: 'Null Rate 0.0%, Cardinality 831 distinct.' Threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: Spot check: SOC 15-1252 (Software Developers) median in [110000, 150000]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_oews WHERE soc_code = '15-1252' AND (wage_annual_median IS NULL OR wage_annual_median < 110000 OR wage_annual_median > 150000)
rules.description: Calibration spot check carried from Bronze RAW-OEWS-008. Silver promote must not corrupt this row.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 2 DQ Rules: 'All Bronze DQ rules still pass.' EDA: 15-1252 median = $133,080, in window.

rules.name: Spot check: SOC 29-1141 (Registered Nurses) median in [75000, 100000]
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_oews WHERE soc_code = '29-1141' AND (wage_annual_median IS NULL OR wage_annual_median < 75000 OR wage_annual_median > 100000)
rules.description: Calibration spot check carried from Bronze RAW-OEWS-009. Silver promote must not corrupt this row.
rules.rationale: Spec §Zone 2 DQ Rules: 'All Bronze DQ rules still pass.' EDA: 29-1141 median = $93,600, in window.

rules.name: All non-null annual wage values are >= 0 (non-negative wage guard, mirrors RAW-OEWS-011)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_oews WHERE (wage_annual_p10 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p10 < 0) OR (wage_annual_p25 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p25 < 0) OR (wage_annual_median IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_median < 0) OR (wage_annual_p75 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p75 < 0) OR (wage_annual_p90 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p90 < 0) OR (wage_annual_mean IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_mean < 0)
rules.description: For every row in base.bls_oews, every non-null annual wage column (wage_annual_p10, wage_annual_p25, wage_annual_median, wage_annual_p75, wage_annual_p90, wage_annual_mean) must be >= 0. Mirrors Bronze RAW-OEWS-011 verbatim against the Silver schema (which uses identical wage column names, per SLV-OEWS-005/008). Closes chaos scenario S10 at the Silver layer so a negative wage cannot survive the Bronze→Silver promote even if the Bronze layer is bypassed during a backfill or replay.
rules.rationale: Per spec §Zone 2 DQ Rules: 'All Bronze DQ rules still pass.' RAW-OEWS-011 added post-chaos as a structural non-negativity guard; this rule replicates the invariant at Silver because the Silver promote is 1:1 (no value transformation), so the same evidence and threshold apply. ID NOTE: requested ID was SLV-OEWS-011, but that ID is already in use (Registered Nurses calibration spot check); per the directive 'do NOT renumber existing rules', this rule is appended as SLV-OEWS-012 with a description tag stating it mirrors RAW-OEWS-011. EDA confirms smallest real wage is $30,160; threshold: 0 violations.

rules.name: total_employment non-negative AND non-null rate >= 99% (mirrors RAW-OEWS-013)
rules.sql: SELECT CASE WHEN neg_count > 0 OR (CAST(non_null AS DOUBLE) / CAST(total AS DOUBLE)) < 0.99 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END AS violation FROM (SELECT COUNT(*) AS total, SUM(CASE WHEN total_employment IS NOT NULL THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS non_null, SUM(CASE WHEN total_employment IS NOT NULL AND total_employment < 0 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) AS neg_count FROM base.bls_oews)
rules.description: Mirrors Bronze RAW-OEWS-013 at the Silver layer. Every non-null `total_employment` value must be >= 0, AND the non-null rate must be >= 99%. The Silver promote is 1:1 (no value transformation), so any regression introduced at Silver (e.g., via a backfill that bypasses Bronze) is caught here. Closes adversarial-auditor gap G1 at the Silver layer.
rules.rationale: Per spec §Zone 2 DQ Rules: 'All Bronze DQ rules still pass.' Adversarial audit gap G1 (P0). EDA confirms 100% non-null and zero negatives in current data; threshold passes cleanly. Defensive at Silver because total_employment is a passthrough field in the 1:1 promote.

rules.name: Annual wage upper-bound sanity (percentiles <= 239200; mean <= 500000) (mirrors RAW-OEWS-014)
rules.sql: SELECT * FROM base.bls_oews WHERE (wage_annual_p10 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p10 > 239200) OR (wage_annual_p25 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p25 > 239200) OR (wage_annual_median IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_median > 239200) OR (wage_annual_p75 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p75 > 239200) OR (wage_annual_p90 IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_p90 > 239200) OR (wage_annual_mean IS NOT NULL AND wage_annual_mean > 500000)
rules.description: Mirrors Bronze RAW-OEWS-014 at the Silver layer. Annual percentile fields are bounded above by the BLS top-code floor of $239,200 by definition; `wage_annual_mean` is bounded by an empirical $500,000 ceiling (16% headroom above observed max of $432,490). Closes adversarial-auditor gap G2 at the Silver layer.
rules.rationale: Per spec §Zone 2 DQ Rules: 'All Bronze DQ rules still pass.' Adversarial audit gap G2 (P0). The Silver promote is 1:1 with no value transformation, so the same upper-bound invariants hold. EDA confirms max non-cap percentile = $216,800; max mean = $432,490 -- threshold passes cleanly.

rules.name: Detailed-only filter held: no summary-group SOC codes present (mirrors RAW-OEWS-015)
rules.description: Mirrors Bronze RAW-OEWS-015 at the Silver layer. No row's `soc_code` may match an OEWS summary-group pattern: `XX-0000` (major), `XX-X000` (minor), or `XX-XX00` (broad). The Silver promote is 1:1 from Bronze; this rule defends against a backfill or replay that bypasses the Bronze filter. Closes adversarial-auditor gap G3 at the Silver layer.
rules.rationale: Per spec §Zone 2 DQ Rules: 'All Bronze DQ rules still pass.' Adversarial audit gap G3 (P0). All 831 rows in current data are detailed SOCs; zero match summary-group regex. Threshold passes cleanly.
